methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

private.name: ClearSpace

data.narrative:
Epsilon3, a Series A space & aerospace company founded by Laura Crabtree, has raised $19M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, spanning similar and adjacent stages.

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $38M. Peer-median anchoring across 4 same-sector comparables at identical or adjacent stage (ClearSpace at $400M Series A; Cowboy Space at $2.0B Series B; Aalyria and Hubble Network each at $400M–$1.3B Series B) produces a median of $850M with a range of $400M–$2.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied; the median Space & Aerospace public multiple is 64.2x across the 5 tickers, but Epsilon3's revenue is not disclosed.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $32M (low) to $977M (high), with a median of $444M. The wide band reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate comparables and capital intensity typical of aerospace ventures. The gap between stage-progression ($38M) and peer-median ($850M) suggests sensitivity to peer selection and stage comparability; resolution depends on Epsilon3's revenue scale and unit economics relative to its Series B-stage peers.
